calendar.matters:
PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Continued To Jan-13-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-18-302258 Estate of Mary A. Melnikoff; PES-18-302256 Estate of Ronald Melnikoff; PES-19-302535 Estate of George A. Melnikoff, Jr.; PES-18-302255 Estate of Robert Jordon and PES-18-302253 Estate of Peter Semenoff.

Appearances: Lisa Tandoc on behalf of all Petitioners.

The Court stated that it seems the best order to do this is to proceed with Nadia first and then go in order from date of death. Ms. Tandoc stated that she has been working with the Probate Examiner, that the Examiner consistently indicated doing Nadia's estate last, referred to an Alameda County case, stated that Alice left a 1/7th share to all of the Siblings and that she believes the Mother's order is recorded. The Court noted that this is really about clearing up title and to do it in any other order does not clean title. Ms. Tandoc stated that Nadia is the only one that distributes to the others, that Alex's was already settled and his order indicates that all of his interest goes to Nicholas, that George Sr.'s estate was opened in 1992, that they found out that George's Administrator died and that they got a Successor Administrator appointed. Ms. Tandoc also stated that George, Jr. passed away, that there is no probate for George, Jr., that there is no probate for Mary, that Mary has a will, but her share is under $150,000.00 and that some of Nadia's heirs are deceased. The Court noted that this is a mess/complicated and spoke of perhaps doing a quiet title action. Ms. Tandoc stated that some of Nadia's Heirs are post-deceased and some are pre-deceased, and that she can do the math to determine everyone's shares, but she needs to figure out how to get there. The Court suggested that looking at Nadia's petition and dealing with the pre-deceased and post-deceased heirs seems to be the cleanest/clearest way. The Petitions to Determine Succession to Real Property (5) are continued to January 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the understanding that only Nadia's estate (302252) will be addressed and that all of the other matters are just trailing for status. Vera's matter (302251) and Nancy's matter (302254) are also continued from September 23, 2019 to January 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Sep-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Continued To Jul-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-93-260733 Estate of George A. Melnikoff;
PES-18-302251 Estate of Vera M. Semenoff; PES-19-302252 Estate of Nadia A. Wilson;
PES-19-302253 Estate of Peter Semenoff; PES-19-302254 Estate of Nancy A. Jordon;
PES-19-302255 Estate of Robert Jordon; PES-19-302256 Estate of Ronald Melnikoff;
PES-18-302258 Estate of Mary A. Melnikoff and PES-19-302535 Estate of George A. Melnikoff, Jr.

Appearances: Lisa Tandoc for Nicholas Melnikoff.

Ms. Tandoc stated that there is no direct probate code regarding a post deceased heir with respect to succession, that all of the estates are under $150,000.00, and that all of the inventory and appraisals have been performed. Ms. Tandoc also spoke of a similar case in Alameda County, stated that she feels they can do something similar, made reference to Probate Code 13151, and stated that all of the named heirs are in agreement and have consented. All Petitions to Determine Succession to Real Property (9) are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
